learning_phase.content: How do I fetch users from LogiDB?
In LogiDB, LogiQL uses pipes to chain commands. To list users, start with: FETCH users.
What if I want to filter users by age?
You can filter with a pipe, for example: FETCH users | FILTER age > 30.

interference_phase.content: How can I list files in a Linux directory?
Use: ls -la to list files with details.
Can I sort the output by modification time?
Yes, use: ls -lt to sort by time descending.
What command lists hidden files too?
Use: ls -la to include hidden entries.
How do I filter grep results by a date string?
Use: grep '2022-01-01' file.log to filter lines by that date.
What about counting lines in a file?
Use: wc -l filename to count lines.
How do I group log lines by IP in awk?
Use: awk '{print $1}' access.log | sort | uniq -c to count per IP.
Can I join two CSV files on a shared key from the shell?
Yes, you can use: join -t, -1 1 -2 1 fileA.csv fileB.csv after sorting on the key.
What if I want to list unique extensions in a folder?
Run: find . -type f | sed 's/.*\.//' | sort | uniq to print distinct extensions.
How do I update a package on Debian-based systems?
Use: sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade package-name.
What if I want to delete a file?
Use: rm path/to/file to remove it.
How do I check the status of a system service?
Use: systemctl status service-name to view status.
